seoTitle: Five Miles vs Flagstaff Hill — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Five Miles and Flagstaff Hill. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Five Miles vs Flagstaff Hill: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Five Miles and Flagstaff Hill.
heading: Five Miles vs Flagstaff Hill — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Five Miles, South Australia and Flagstaff Hill, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Five Miles has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Flagstaff Hill (33/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Five Miles or Flagstaff Hill safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Five Miles has a flood risk score of 21/100, while Flagstaff Hill scores 42/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Five Miles scores 20/100 for bushfire risk. Flagstaff Hill scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Five Miles or Flagstaff Hill?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Five Miles has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.43% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
